phase_0_import_truth.note: ModuleNotFoundError resolved prior to F206AC work

phase_4_isolated_fetch.note: aiohttp WITH curl_cffi disabled works correctly — 200, text present, 12KB

phase_4_isolated_fetch_404.note: aiohttp WITH curl_cffi disabled works — 404 is valid HTTP, body returned

suspected_root_cause: httpx_h2 transport path failure — when curl_cffi is DISABLED and httpx_h2 is ENABLED, the httpx_h2 path is used for all public fetches. Isolated test with httpx_h2 disabled (forcing aiohttp directly) shows aiohttp works perfectly (200, text). The httpx_h2 transport may be failing silently or corrupting connection state, causing ALL 10 fetches to fail. This is NOT a discovery problem (10 hits returned), NOT a content-type problem (aiohttp gets text), and NOT a circuit breaker at discovery stage. Root cause is in the httpx_h2 multiplexed connection management.
key_findings: Isolated aiohttp fetch WORKS: 200 status, 12KB XML text, no errors
httpx_h2 ENABLED in F206AB run — all 10 fetches failed
aiohttp works in isolation — httpx_h2 is the likely culprit
fetch_error_types and fetch_error_samples added to public_branch_verdict
No behavior changes to fetch decisions
recommendation: A) httpx_h2 connection management investigation — httpx_h2 multiplexed connections may be in a bad state. Consider: (1) httpx_h2 connection pool reset between fetches, (2) adding httpx_h2 circuit breaker similar to curl_cffi, (3) reverting to aiohttp as default when curl_cffi is disabled. The isolated fetch proves aiohttp works fine; the problem is specifically with the httpx_h2 path in the pipeline context.
